headline: Buy, Sell or Hold NFLX Stock After the Dip?
summary: At $92.37, Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) is a Hold. The stock has been a top-tier compounder for a decade, but the post-earnings dip has not made it cheap enough to chase when better-priced opportunities exist. Netflix runs the world’s largest paid streaming service with 325 million plus subscribers. Q1 2026 revenue grew 16.2% YoY to $12.25 billion ... Buy, Sell or Hold NFLX Stock After the Dip?

headline: Netflix Shareholder Activism Puts Governance And Valuation In Focus
summary: Netflix (NasdaqGS:NFLX) is facing an unusually high volume of shareholder proposals ahead of its June 2026 annual meeting. Items on the ballot span cumulative voting, expanded consent rights, and greater board accountability. Investors are also asking for more detailed reporting on legal, regulatory, and ESG related investment risks. This cluster of governance and transparency proposals reflects rising pressure from a broad mix of stakeholders. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:NFLX, this...
